channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Why European backlash over Trump intervention won't worry Infantino
item.description: After 10 years as Fifa president, could the Folarin Balogun controversy tip the balance against Gianni Infantino? Don't bet on it.

item.title: FA considering appeal after Quansah red card
item.description: The Football Association is considering whether to appeal after England defender Jarell Quansah's red card against Mexico.

item.title: James expected to return for Norway quarter-final
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel expects Reece James to be available for the World Cup quarter-final against Norway on Saturday.

item.title: Mbappe condemns racist posts from Paraguay senator
item.description: France forward Kylian Mbappe condemns a Paraguayan senator as "despicable" and unworthy of her position after she made a number of racist remarks mocking his origin and education.

item.title: Tottenham seal £100m move for Newcastle's Tonali
item.description: Tottenham complete the signing of midfielder Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United for a club-record fee which could reach £100m.

item.title: How England used five mini games to beat Mexico
item.description: The tactical breakdown of how Thomas Tuchel's 10-man England pulled off a remarkable World Cup win in a hostile Mexico City.

item.title: Starmer intervened in row over Mexico-England kick-off time
item.description: Fifa had been set to bring the match forward by six hours, due to a forecast of thunderstorms.

item.title: Henderson expected to have surgery on injured wrist
item.description: Jordan Henderson is expected to have surgery on the wrist injury he sustained while celebrating England's win over Mexico on Monday.

item.title: An incredible night when England heroes were made
item.description: On a spine-tingling night of drama, emotion and pure theatre in one of sport's most atmospheric arenas, England delivered one of their greatest performances.

item.title: Spain into quarter-finals after Merino's late winner against Portugal
item.description: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.

item.title: Super-sub Merino delivers 'killer goal' for Spain
item.description: Spain midfielder Mikel Merino coolly scores in added time to break Portuguese hearts, sending his side through to the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: How England's epic night at the Azteca unfolded
item.description: Relive all the drama from England’s 3-2 win over Mexico, as Thomas Tuchel’s team progressed to a quarter-final meeting with Norway on Saturday.

item.title: England win epic tie against Mexico to reach quarter-finals
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores twice and Harry Kane converts a penalty as 10-man England beat Mexico 3-2 in the World Cup.

item.title: Bellingham scores two in 98 seconds to silence the Azteca
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores two goals in 98 seconds to give England a 2-0 lead over Mexico during their last 16 match at the Mexico City Stadium.

item.title: 'Nasty, nasty challenge' - Quansah shown red card
item.description: England are reduced to 10 men as Jarrel Quansah is shown a red card following a VAR review for a "nasty" challenge on Mexico's Jesus Gallardo.

item.title: Where does Mexico win rank among England's best results?
item.description: BBC Sport looks at some of England's most famous away victories after their epic win against Mexico at the Azteca - plus you can have your say.

item.title: After a year of ICE raids, Southern California finds joy in Mexico's World Cup run
item.description: For Mexico's football-loving community, the World Cup has been a triumph, despite their last-16 exit. The joy has spread to Southern California, home to one of the largest Mexican communities outside Mexico.

item.title: Glasner becomes Forest's fifth head coach in a year
item.description: Former Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner replaces Vitor Pereira following his sacking last week.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 29
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Who produced another iconic performance? England player ratings
item.description: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell rates how the Three Lions players performed in the last-16 win over Mexico.

item.title: Coach told FA Maddy Cusack was a liar, inquest hears
item.description: Jonathan Morgan says she left out detail about their relationship when speaking to her family.

item.title: Brentford set to sign Wilson after West Ham exit
item.description: Brentford are set to sign former England striker Callum Wilson on an initial 12-month contract after his exit from relegated West Ham.

item.title: Tottenham to bid for Kroupi - Tuesday gossip
item.description: Tottenham to target Bournemouth's Kroupi, Atletico Madrid a possible home for Salah, Wan-Bissaka a potential White replacement for Arsenal, plus more.

item.title: Arsenal sign Germany striker Cerci on free
item.description: Arsenal have confirmed the signing of Germany striker Selina Cerci from Hoffenheim on a free transfer.

item.title: Neymar says Brazil career over after Norway defeat
item.description: An emotional Neymar says his international career for Brazil is "over" following his side's exit at the 2026 World Cup at the hands of Norway.

item.title: 'Iconic match at iconic stadium' - England win down to 'pure will'
item.description: England are praised for showing "heart and belief" as they beat Mexico in an "iconic match at an iconic stadium" to reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: England into quarter-finals - what is their path to the final?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after progressing the quarter-finals.

item.title: Red card system in disarray over Trump, Fifa and Balogun decision
item.description: The decision to effectively overturn the red card shown to United States forward Folarin Balogun leaves many unanswered questions.

item.title: The numbers behind Spain defence chasing World Cup history
item.description: With Spain yet to concede at the 2026 World Cup and on the brink of another tournament record, BBC Sport examines the numbers behind the shutouts.

item.title: 'I'm speechless' - Kane reacts to England's victory over Mexico
item.description: A very hoarse sounding England captain Harry Kane gives his reaction to England's 3-2 victory over Mexico and praises the fans support.

item.title: Haaland sends Norway into quarter-finals as Brazil crash out
item.description: Erling Haaland scores twice for Norway as they beat Brazil 2-1 at the New York New Jersey Stadium to reach the quarter-finals of the World Cup for the first time.

item.title: What Haaland's home town did when Norway beat Brazil
item.description: Watch how Erling Haaland's hometown of Bryne reacted to their hero scoring both goals as Norway knocked out Brazil to advance to the quarter-final of the World Cup.

item.title: Haaland double fires Norway into World Cup quarter-finals
item.description: Erling Haaland scores twice as Norway knock out five-time champions Brazil, and progress to the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: How 'remarkable' Haaland lead Norway to win over Brazil
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney, Joe Hart, and Micah Richards look at how "absolute monster" Erling Haaland inspired Norway to victory over Brazil in the last 16 of the World Cup.

item.title: 'Sportsmanship in question' after decision to suspend Balogun red card
item.description: BBC Sport World Cup pundits Wayne Rooney, Joe Hart, and Micah Richards react to the decision to suspend the red card shown to the USA's Florian Balogun during their round of 32 tie with Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: 'Disgusting' Paraguay went 'way too far' against France
item.description: BBC pundits Thomas Hitzlsperger, Joe Hart and Micah Richards discuss Paraguay's attempts to get under France's skin at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: France overcome resolute Paraguay to reach quarter-final
item.description: World Cup favourites France book their quarter-final place thanks to Kylian Mbappe's second-half penalty against a stubborn Paraguay side in Philadelphia.

item.title: The man England will fear most - Haaland fires Norway to history
item.description: Erling Haaland has scored seven goals in four World Cup games and he is the striker that nobody wants to face as he fires Norway into an historic quarter-final.

item.title: Parkinson's five great years at Wrexham but work to do
item.description: Wrexham's boss is the longest serving Championship manager but insists there is a "lot more work" ahead.

item.title: Ageing Brazil need major surgery - but is Ancelotti the man to do it?
item.description: There was nothing unlucky about Brazil's loss to Norway in the last 16 of the World Cup and their exit at this stage is an unmitigated disaster, writes Tim Vickery.

item.title: Newcastle sign Toure for £43m - what will he bring to club?
item.description: Newcastle United reporter Ciaran Kelly looks at why the club have bought forward Bazoumana Toure from Hoffenheim for £43m.

item.title: Quiz: Name every team England have played in a World Cup quarter-final
item.description: Norway will be the 11th team England will have faced in a World Cup quarter-final - so can you name the other 10?

item.title: Brazil going out? Chris Sutton predicts World Cup's last-16 matches
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-16 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against Mexico.

item.title: Has Glasner tarnished Palace legacy with move to new enemies Forest?
item.description: Will Oliver Glasner tarnish Crystal Palace legacy with Nottingham Forest move?

item.title: Will Irish League clubs make more European history?
item.description: European football has become a stage where Irish Premiership teams can dream bigger than ever, but will any clubs achieve more history this season?

item.title: Brentford set to sign Burnley forward Anthony
item.description: Brentford are nearing a move for Burnley forward Jaidon Anthony for a £15m fee rising to £17m with add-ons.

item.title: What are the games to look out for in the Scottish League Cup?
item.description: Four top-flight clubs open their competitive season in the group stages of the Scottish League Cup on Saturday.

item.title: Chelsea's Santos considered as Man Utd look to solve midfield issues
item.description: After missing out on Elliot Anderson and Mateus Fernandes, are Bournemouth's Alex Scott and Chelsea's Andrey Santos now heading Man Utd's list of midfield targets?

item.title: Eight of the 10 most populous countries are not in the World Cup
item.description: Many countries with massive populations consistently miss out on football’s biggest party. What can they do to change their sporting fortunes?

item.title: Thousands welcome home Cape Verde players after stunning World Cup run
item.description: Tens of thousands of fans formed an ocean of blue outside the airport in capital city, Praia.

item.title: Queiroz leaves Ghana following World Cup exit
item.description: Carlos Queiroz steps down as Ghana head coach after three months in charge following their World Cup exit.

item.title: Murals, flags and beef dishes - Messi mania takes hold in Miami
item.description: Lionel Messi's World Cup goals roadshow continued with his seventh of the tournament for Argentina against Cape Verde - and delighted the fans who were in Miami to see their hero.

item.title: Farewell Cape Verde - the underdogs the World Cup will never forget
item.description: Cape Verde may have been the World Cup's second-smallest nation but they leave the largest impact as they depart following defeat by defending champions Argentina in an epic.

item.title: After World Cup goals are last year's signings finally set to deliver for Newcastle?
item.description: Anthony Elanga and Yoane Wissa both struggled in their first season at Newcastle, but five goals between at the World Cup them suggest things will be different next term.

item.title: First Afcon, now World Cup - Senegal trapped in 'football hell'
item.description: Senegal's nightmarish year continues, with a heart-breaking collapse against Belgium featuring late penalty drama which was remarkably similar to events in this year's chaotic Afcon final.

item.title: Why Spurs' statement spending appears set to continue
item.description: Tottenham are set to break their record transfer for the second time in a matter of days with the club's impressive performance off the field allowing greater flexibility for investment into the first-team squad.

item.title: USA march on but will Balogun red card prove costly?
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino's USA side set up a World Cup last-16 meeting with Belgium, but how costly will forward Folarin Balogun's red card prove?

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: As Norway advance, is Wright correct about Scottish football's ills?
item.description: From comparisons with Norway, undervalued broadcasting deals and unfulfilled potential, Ian Wright says he feels sorry for Scottish football fans - but is the former England striker right?

item.title: Bologna keen on Rangers' Barron amid possible Ferguson swap - gossip
item.description: Rangers midfielders linked with moves, speculation over Celtic targets and St Johnstone to offer winger trial.

item.title: Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?
item.description: Your one-stop shop for all the ins and outs in the Scottish Premiership during the 2026 summer transfer window.

item.title: Beever-Jones signs new contract until 2030 with Chelsea
item.description: England forward Aggie Beever-Jones signs a new contract with Chelsea, extending her stay at the club she joined as a nine-year-old until 2030.

item.title: Arsenal sign Lioness Stanway from Bayern
item.description: England midfielder Georgia Stanway says she is joining Arsenal to "win trophies and grow as a player".

item.title: Denmark defender Thomsen signs two-year London City deal
item.description: London City Lionesses sign Denmark international defender Janni Thomsen from Utah Royals FC.

item.title: Chelsea sign Japan midfielder Matsukubo
item.description: Chelsea sign Japan international Manaka Matsukubo on a five-year contract from North Carolina Courage.

item.title: Aston Villa sign Norway midfielder Kielland
item.description: Norway midfielder Justine Kielland joins Aston Villa from German club Wolfsburg, subject to visa approval. Kielland joined Norwegian side Brann in 2023. During her time there, she helped the club reach the quarter-finals of the Women's Champions League, playing in 12 matches and scoring four goals.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
